title: Persea bootanica
taxonomy: Persea bootanica is classified within the family Lauraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the laurel family. This family consists of about 85 genera and over 2,000 species. Persea bootanica belongs to the genus Persea, which is a group of evergreen trees and shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Persea is further divided into several species, with Persea bootanica being one of the lesser-known species within this group. Persea bootanica is a member of the Laurales order, which also includes other flowering plant families such as the Sassafras and the Cinnamomum.
uses: Persea bootanica is a species of tree known to have several medicinal uses. Its bark, leaves, and seeds are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. In traditional medicine, the plant's extracts are used to reduce inflammation and as an antiseptic. Additionally, it is believed to have antimicrobial properties that help combat infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Persea bootanica's medicinal uses.

parts: Persea bootanica, commonly known as the Nepalese bay tree, is used in traditional medicine. The medicinal parts of the plant include its leaves, bark, and roots. intro: Persea bootanica, also known as the Himalayan avocado, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Himalayas.
